name: apple-health-mcp-server
description: Probably the most complete Apple Health MCP server — local-first ingest of Apple Health export.xml + ECG CSVs + GPX into DuckDB, exposed over 18 read-oriented MCP tools.
long_description: Ingests an Apple Health export (export.xml + electrocardiograms/*.csv + workout-routes/*.gpx) into a local DuckDB database and exposes 18 read-oriented MCP tools over it (records / workouts / heart rate samples / ECG readings / activity summaries / state of mind / etc., plus a validated SELECT-only escape hatch). All data stays local; nothing is uploaded.

db_path.title: Database file
db_path.description: Path to the DuckDB database file holding your imported Apple Health data. On Windows, Claude Desktop runs inside an MSIX AppContainer sandbox that redirects %LOCALAPPDATA%, so the server cannot see the DB the importer wrote to under the system path. Pointing this entry at a stable location outside the sandbox (for example C:\Users\<you>\apple-health\health.duckdb) lets both the terminal importer and Claude Desktop's server read the same file. On macOS / Linux the platform default is fine; leave this empty to fall back to ~/.local/share/apple-health-mcp/health.duckdb.
